name: Statamic Flat-File CMS REST API
what_it_does: Laravel-based flat-file CMS with REST and GraphQL APIs — enabling developers to manage content in Git-tracked flat files (YAML, Markdown) with a beautiful control panel, deliver content via REST API or GraphQL for headless use, with a flexible field system, live preview, multi-site support, and the power of Laravel for custom functionality.
use_cases: Delivering headless content via Statamic REST API for decoupled frontend applications
Managing Statamic entries via API for content migration and automation workflows
Querying Statamic collections and taxonomies via GraphQL for type-safe frontend queries
Triggering Statamic webhooks on content events for CI/CD pipeline integration
Managing Statamic assets via API for media library automation
not_for: Teams without PHP/Laravel expertise (Statamic extends Laravel)
High-traffic sites with millions of content items (flat-file model has scaling limits)
Teams needing a fully managed SaaS CMS without server management
best_when: A PHP/Laravel developer or agency needs a beautiful, Git-based CMS with headless API capabilities — where content lives in version-controlled flat files, and the power of Laravel enables unlimited customization.
avoid_when: You don't have PHP/Laravel expertise, need database-backed scalability for millions of records, or want managed SaaS without server admin.

auth.notes: API token auth via X-Statamic-Token header. Tokens created in Statamic control panel. Public read endpoint configurable per collection. Private write API behind auth. Webhook for content events (publish, update, delete). GraphQL via Statamic GraphQL addon. Laravel Sanctum for user auth. No OAuth2. Role-based access control via Statamic roles system. Self-hosted so developer controls all auth.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for 1 user (Solo) — full features, 1 admin user
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro one-time $259/project (unlimited users); Statamic Cloud from $19/month (managed hosting)
pricing.notes: Connecticut company. Founded 2012 by Jack McDade and Jason Vongphrachanh. Bootstrapped and profitable. PHP/Laravel CMS. Free for solo developers (1 user, full features). Pro license $259 one-time per project (unlimited users). Built on Laravel (PHP) framework. Flat-file storage by default (YAML, Markdown, Git-trackable). Optional database support (Eloquent drivers). Beautiful control panel (Blueprint field system). Live preview. Multi-site. Statamic Cloud for managed hosting. 25,000+ Statamic sites. Very popular in agency and marketing site world.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST + GraphQL APIs. API token auth via X-Statamic-Token. Webhook for content events. Good developer documentation. Self-hosted so user controls all. No rate limits from platform (server-dependent). Free for solo use. PHP/Laravel expertise required. Open source core.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All content reads idempotent by entry ID or slug. Collection reads idempotent. Asset reads idempotent. Write operations idempotent if same ID used.
known_agent_gotchas: PHP/Laravel expertise required — not for non-PHP developers
Flat-file model — performance degrades with very large content sets (10K+ entries)
REST API must be explicitly enabled in config (not on by default)
Public API and authenticated API are separate — configure per-collection
Self-hosted — agents need network access to the Statamic server

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS depends on user's server config. User-controlled data residency and compliance. API token via X-Statamic-Token header. Per-collection public/private API configuration. Laravel Sanctum for user authentication. RBAC via Statamic roles. Git-tracked content enables security audit trail. Open source — auditable. No OAuth2 or fine-grained API scopes. Self-hosted — user responsible for server security.
